title: adenandra marginata subsp. humilis
intro: Adenandra marginata subsp. humilis, also known as the 'Cape Hazel' or 'Humilis Adenandra', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils. The health benefits of Adenandra marginata subsp. humilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water to produce a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
